title: Fix token bucket that overfills after idle time
prompt: ratelimit.py implements a TokenBucket(capacity, refill_rate) with allow(now). It refills tokens based on elapsed time and consumes one per allowed request. The bug: the refill is not capped at capacity, so after a long idle period the bucket accumulates far more tokens than capacity and lets a big burst through. Fix allow() so the token count never exceeds capacity, while still refilling correctly over time.
prompt_vague: The TokenBucket in ratelimit.py lets too many requests through. Fix it.
prompt_paraphrase: TokenBucket(capacity, refill_rate) in ratelimit.py tops up tokens according to elapsed time and spends one per allowed call, but it forgets to clamp the total at capacity, so a long idle gap lets a burst much larger than capacity succeed. Cap the refill at capacity while keeping the time-based refill behavior.
